title: Well-formed vegeta breakpoint test — step-increase rate, p99 SLO check, warmup
code_snippet:
#!/bin/bash
# breakpoint.sh — find service ceiling via vegeta
SLO_P99_MS=200

# Warmup (discard)
echo "GET http://api/endpoint" | vegeta attack -rate=50/s -duration=15s > /dev/null

for rate in 100 200 500 1000 2000 5000; do
  echo "Testing at ${rate} RPS..."
  p99=$(echo "GET http://api/endpoint" | \
    vegeta attack -rate=${rate}/s -duration=30s | \
    vegeta report -type=json | \
    jq '.latencies."99th"' | \
    awk '{printf "%.0f", $1/1000000}')
  echo "  p99 = ${p99}ms (SLO: ${SLO_P99_MS}ms)"
  if [ "$p99" -gt "$SLO_P99_MS" ]; then
    echo "BREAKPOINT: p99 exceeded SLO at ${rate} RPS"
    break
  fi
done
expected_feedback: No violations. Correctly implements vegeta breakpoint test: (1) warmup phase discarded, (2) step-increase rate pattern, (3) p99 SLO threshold check per step, (4) stops at breakpoint, (5) uses constant-rate attack for precise RPS control.
